stem: High-level code can be translated into machine code using a compiler or an interpreter.
model_answer: A compiler translates the whole program into machine code in one go and produces an executable file that can be run again without re-translation. This makes compiled programs fast to run but slower to develop because small changes require a rebuild, and the executable only runs on the type of processor it was compiled for. An interpreter reads and executes the source code one line (or statement) at a time; nothing is saved, so it must retranslate every run, which is slower at run-time but makes it easy to test changes immediately during development.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Compare a compiler and an interpreter.

mark_points.text: compiler translates the whole program in one go, producing an executable
interpreter translates and executes one line/statement at a time
accepted_alternatives: interpreter runs code line by line

mark_points.text: compiled programs run faster than interpreted programs / interpreter must translate on every run
accepted_alternatives: interpreted programs are slower at runtime

mark_points.text: interpreter makes development/debugging easier (can run changes immediately); compiled executable is platform-specific
accepted_alternatives: interpreter reports errors quickly / no rebuild needed
compiled code only runs on target CPU/OS
